decision: Migrated src/middleware.ts to src/proxy.ts for Next.js 16 compatibility
why: Next.js 16 deprecated the middleware.ts file convention in favor of proxy.ts. The runtime export was removed because proxy.ts does not support the runtime config option (it throws an error); Node.js is now the default runtime (stable since Next.js 15.5). Auth logic is unchanged — auth() wrapper from NextAuth v5 is still used as the default export, with the same matcher config.

decision: Store fieldState (pending/accepted/rejected) inside the FormField JSON blob in the fields column rather than adding a new DB column
why: No schema migration needed; field-level review state is intrinsically part of the field data already stored in that JSON blob. PATCH route merges it on save.

decision: Export getClient() from analyze-form.ts as a shared singleton for all AI route handlers
why: analyze-web/route.ts was instantiating its own Anthropic client, creating a second singleton. Exporting getClient() from analyze-form.ts centralizes the singleton and ensures ANTHROPIC_API_KEY validation happens in one place.

decision: Sanitize WebField label/id/placeholder with PROMPT_INJECTION_PATTERN before prompt interpolation
why: These fields are user-controlled strings injected verbatim into the Claude system prompt. Stripping HTML tags, instruction delimiters (###, ---), role-switch keywords (system:, user:), and zero-width Unicode characters closes the prompt injection vector identified in the security audit (issue #23).
